creator: grog@lemis.com
text:
Installation of Netscape Communicator 4.5 (international
	version) with 128 bit encryption fails with multiple problems
	in Fortify install:

	PROBLEM 1
	---------

	#  make install -DUSE_128BIT 
	>> Checksum OK for communicator-v45-export.x86-unknown-freebsd.tar.gz.
	>> No checksum recorded for Fortify-1.3.2-unix-x86.tar.gz.
	Make sure the Makefile and md5 file (/T/FreeBSD/www/netscape45-communicator/files/md5)
	are up to date.  If you want to override this check, type
	"make NO_CHECKSUM=yes [other args]".
	*** Error code 1

	PROBLEM 2
	---------

	Repeating, 

	# make NO_CHECKSUM=yes install -DUSE_128BIT 
	===>  Extracting for netscape-communicator-4.5
	...
	/bin/mv -f /usr/local/netscape-4.5/netscape /usr/local/netscape-4.5/communicator-4.5.bin
	install -c -o root -g wheel -m 555 /T/FreeBSD/www/netscape45-communicator/work/communicator-v45.x86-unknown-freebsd/netscape /usr/local/bin/communicator-4.5
	/bin/ln -sf communicator-4.5 /usr/local/bin/communicator-4
	/bin/ln -sf communicator-4 /usr/local/bin/netscape
	if [ ! -f /usr/local/netscape-4.5/mailcap ] ; then  install -c -o root -g wheel -m 444 /T/FreeBSD/www/netscape45-communicator/files/mailcap /usr/local/netscape-4.5;  fi
	/bin/rm -rf /usr/local/netscape-4.5/movemail-src
	cd /T/FreeBSD/www/netscape45-communicator/work/Fortify-1.3.2-unix-x86 &&  (echo yes ; echo no) | ./Fortify.sh /usr/local/netscape-4.5/communicator-4.5.bin
	====   Fortify 1.3.2; Copyright (C) 1997,1998 Farrell McKay   ====
	==  This software is free for all forms of non-commercial use.  ==
	
	"/usr/local/netscape-4.5/communicator-4.5.bin" is ....
	.... Netscape Communicator 4.5 for x86-unknown-freebsd (Export grade) 
	
	Do you wish to fortify
	"/usr/local/netscape-4.5/communicator-4.5.bin" [yes|no|quit] ?
	Do you wish to keep a backup copy of
	"/usr/local/netscape-4.5/communicator-4.5.bin" [yes|no|quit] ?
	Congratulations!
	"/usr/local/netscape-4.5/communicator-4.5.bin" has been
	successfully fortified.
	
	Do you wish to test this browser [yes|no|quit] ? Do you wish
	to test this browser [yes|no|quit] ? Do you wish to test this
	browser [yes|no|quit] ? Do you wish to test this browser
	[yes|no|quit] ? Do you wish to test this browser [yes|no|quit]
	? Do you wish to test this browser [yes|no|quit]
	
	This last section represents the install process looping,
	filling screen after screen of text.

	PROBLEM 1
	---------

	Repeating, 

	# make NO_CHECKSUM=yes install -DUSE_128BIT 
	===>  Installing for netscape-communicator-4.5
	cd /T/FreeBSD/www/netscape45-communicator/work/communicator-v45.x86-unknown-freebsd &&  yes "" | LC_TIME=C MOZILLA_HOME="/usr/local/netscape-4.5" ./ns-install
	./ns-install: not found
	*** Error code 127
	
	Stop.
	*** Error code 1

	It was necessary to remove the work/.[a-z] files (or at least
	one of them) to repeat.

	PROBLEM 2
	---------

	After installation without Fortify,

	# netscape&
	[1] 63516
	=== root@freebie (/dev/ttyp7) /usr/ports/www/netscape45-communicator 149 -> exec: /usr/local/lib/netscape/communicator-4.5.bin: not found
	echo $MOZILLA_HOME 
	/usr/local/lib/netscape
	
	PROBLEM 3
	---------

	After resetting MOZILLA_HOME,

	netscape&
	communicator-4_5_bin: program is version 4.5, but resources are version 4.04.

        This means that there is an inappropriate `Netscape' file installed
        in the app-defaults directory.  Check these environment variables
        and the directories to which they point:

        $XAPPLRESDIR
        $XFILESEARCHPATH
        $XUSERFILESEARCHPATH

        Also check for this file in your home directory, or in the
        directory called `app-defaults' somewhere under /usr/lib/.

	PROBLEM 4
	---------

	# mv /usr/local/netscape-4.5/Netscape.ad /usr/X11R6/lib/X11/app-defaults/Netscape
	netscape
	ERROR: bad magic number in "/usr/local/netscape-4.5/plugins/cpPack1.jar.old"
	Cant load plugin /usr/local/netscape-4.5/plugins/cpPack1.jar.old. Ignored.

Fix: 

Problem 1: It looks as if one of the stamp files has been
	allocated too early.  It should probably  be removed on
	failure of the Fortify install.

	Problem 2: Check for MOZILLA_HOME, warn if it's set to
	something else.  This can only be a partial solution, of
	course, since other users can still get bitten.  A warning
	message should also be printed.

	Problem 3: Install the app-defaults in the right place.  I
	wasn't able to find information about the purposes of the
	three X environment variables, but in FreeBSD it doesn't seem
	appropriate to have the application default files stored
	anywhere except in /usr/X11R6/lib/X11/app-defaults.

	Problem 4: This is probably a bug in Netscrape, not the
	installation.  Ignore.
How-To-Repeat: 
	As shown above
